codeReview.summary: Fail. The feature added a substantial Foundry deployment script and role wiring, but it missed one explicit deploy-script requirement and the reviewed commit did not actually provide a working end-to-end PVM deployment path.

issues.description: The feature description explicitly required reusing existing `WPAS` and `USDCMock` when bytecode is already present, but the script still unconditionally does `new WPAS()` and `new USDCMock(...)` with no code-existence check or override path. That violates the requested behavior and makes the deployment non-idempotent for the market assets.

The reviewed commit did not actually complete the requested PVM deployment support. `DeployPVM.sh` tells the operator to redeploy with `QUOTE_ENGINE_ADDRESS=$PVM_CONTRACT_ADDRESS`, but in commit `484924e` `dualvm/script/Deploy.s.sol:206-208` hard-coded `RiskGateway` to the freshly deployed EVM `DeterministicRiskModel` and had no env/input for a PVM quote-engine address. So the feature shipped a PVM deploy wrapper that could not be wired into the canonical system without a follow-up code change.

The shell wrapper's printed next-step instructions are now stale: it still says to use `QUOTE_ENGINE_ADDRESS=...`, while the current `Deploy.s.sol` expects `PVM_QUOTE_ENGINE_ADDRESS`. An operator following the wrapper literally will use the wrong env var and get the EVM quote engine unless they already know the later fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `solidity-worker` skill does not mention Foundry's sensitive-value censoring when deployment scripts write addresses to JSON manifests, even though this feature hit that exact pitfall and had to invent a workaround mid-flight.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md`:46-50 only says to output addresses to console or JSON; the handoff at `handoffs/2026-03-19T03-56-23-399Z__forge-deploy-script__8d490a8a-e4d7-468e-a9d3-d94c7967035f.json`:59-72 documents the GovernanceToken censoring issue and explicitly suggests adding this note to the skill.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state docs are stale about Foundry deployment configuration. `AGENTS.md` still describes a simpler `foundry.toml` and a plain `forge script` deployment command, but live M11 deployment now requires the dedicated deploy profile and extra flags documented elsewhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md`:34 says `libs=["node_modules"]` and line 77 gives a generic `forge script script/Deploy.s.sol --rpc-url $RPC_URL --broadcast --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY`; current `dualvm/foundry.toml`:18-31 adds `[profile.deploy]` with `via_ir=false`, and `.factory/library/environment.md`:48-50 says `FOUNDRY_PROFILE=deploy` is required with `--legacy --gas-estimate-multiplier 500 --slow`.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `484924e`, and the current versions of `dualvm/script/Deploy.s.sol`, `dualvm/script/DeployPVM.sh`, and `dualvm/foundry.toml`. The feature should fail scrutiny because it never implemented the explicitly requested asset-reuse behavior and the reviewed commit's PVM wrapper was not actually usable to deploy the canonical system with a PVM quote engine.
